codeReview.summary: The documentation feature delivers all four expected outputs: README.md with installation/quickstart/benchmark sections, SKILL.md language field fix from PascalCase to lowercase, all 5 ADRs confirmed in Accepted status, and a comprehensive recommendation memo covering architecture rationale, rejected paths, open risks, and next steps. The diff touches 3 files (README.md new, SKILL.md 1-line fix, docs/recommendation-memo.md new) totaling 335 insertions. Content is well-structured, data-backed, and internally consistent with benchmark reports. Two minor non-blocking inaccuracies were found.

issues.description: Placeholder URL 'https://github.com/your-org/vera.git' in the install instructions. Should be replaced with the actual repository URL when the project is published. Acceptable for an unreleased project but will confuse users if shipped as-is.

Technology stack table says 'Rust 1.94' which is the installed compiler version, not the minimum supported version. Cargo.toml specifies rust-version = '1.85' and README correctly says 'Rust 1.85+'. The memo should say 'Rust (MSRV 1.85)' or simply 'Rust' without a specific version to avoid version confusion.

Opening line references '21-task evaluation suite across 4 repositories' but the README and benchmark reports consistently reference '17 tasks across 3 repositories'. The 21-task count includes turborepo tasks that were excluded (explained later in the memo at line 85), but the discrepancy in the opening paragraph may confuse readers. Consider '21-task evaluation suite (17 completed)' or matching the README's language.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used implementation-worker skill for a documentation-only feature and correctly deviated from test-first development (Step 2). The skill procedure doesn't account for documentation-only features that have no testable Rust code. Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges recommends adding a note about this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Skipped test-first since this feature is purely documentation'. The implementation-worker SKILL.md Step 2 says 'Write Failing Tests First' with no exception for non-code features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: README uses placeholder git clone URL (https://github.com/your-org/vera.git). AGENTS.md doesn't document the actual repository URL or specify conventions for placeholder URLs in documentation. If the project has a real repo URL, it should be captured somewhere so documentation workers can use it.

summary: PASS. All four expected behaviors are satisfied: README has installation/quickstart/benchmarks sections, SKILL.md language field corrected to lowercase 'rust', all 5 ADRs in Accepted status, and recommendation memo covers architecture rationale, rejected paths, open risks, and next steps. Three non-blocking issues identified: placeholder git URL in README, Rust version inconsistency between memo and Cargo.toml, and task-count discrepancy between memo opening and actual benchmark results. Worker ran full verification suite (cargo test, clippy, fmt, all CLI --help commands) and committed cleanly.
